characters.description: A classless survivor, protector of Donnyton, and disciple of Shal. His unusual plant, magic, and spear abilities make him one of the settlement's principal defenders.
characters.name: Randidly Ghosthound

characters.description: Randidly's Spear Phantom mentor, whose demanding instruction links strength to responsibility. He has left Earth, but Randidly still owes him aid.
characters.name: Shal

characters.description: A Donnyton mage and trusted associate of Randidly. She contributes to the settlement's defense and government.
characters.name: Lyra

characters.description: Donnyton's village spirit and a central part of the settlement's established System functions.
characters.name: Nul

characters.description: Donnyton's chieftain and a frontline defender responsible for the settlement and its residents.
characters.name: Donny

characters.description: A Donnyton administrator who helps manage the settlement's policy and practical affairs.
characters.name: Daniel

characters.description: A Killer-class squad leader who directs one of Donnyton's specialized combat teams.
characters.name: Decklan

characters.description: A heavy Donnyton fighter and pet handler who is partnered with Annie.
characters.name: Dozer

characters.description: An archer, senior Donnyton defender, and Dozer's partner.
characters.name: Annie

characters.description: A Donnyton smith who supports the settlement through his craft.
characters.name: Sam

characters.description: A healer and member of Donnyton's leadership.
characters.name: Regina

characters.description: A classless fighter and field operator connected to Donnyton.
characters.name: Alana

characters.description: A scarred artist among the Frankfurd refugees.
characters.name: Raina

characters.description: Randidly's blood-feeding soul plant and an ally of Donnyton.
characters.name: Thorn

characters.description: Randidly's support-oriented soul plant and part of the plant-based power he carries from his earlier survival.
characters.name: Arbor

characters.description: An ambitious member of Decklan's Killer squad.
characters.name: Ivan

characters.description: One of Randidly's two missing friends from before the System and an unresolved search objective.
characters.name: Ace

characters.description: The second of Randidly's two missing friends from before the System. Their disappearance remains a personal obligation rather than a closed loss, although Randidly begins this volume without a reliable location or confirmation that either missing friend survived.
characters.name: Randidly's other missing friend

characters.description: Randidly's missing friend from before the System. A three-person search party has gone north to investigate leads, but Sydney's whereabouts and safety remain unresolved.
characters.name: Sydney

characters.description: Randidly's former girlfriend, now remaining in Frankfurd rather than relocating to Donnyton. She confronts him over their separation and an alleged betrayal involving one of his missing friends, a charge he denies.
characters.name: Tessa

characters.description: A hostile shapeshifting entity that first approaches Lyra in Randidly Ghosthound's form and pressures her to act as its guide.
characters.name: Shapeshifting tribulation

characters.description: A yeti-like judgment sent to punish Randidly for his Heretic status. It confronts him with smaller frost creatures under its command.
characters.name: Frostbite judgment

characters.description: The neighboring settlement's former king, now incapacitated and held as a bound prisoner in the leadership residence.
characters.name: Deposed king

characters.description: A former member of the survivor group who voluntarily supports the neighboring settlement's new faction and is not being held as the deposed king's captive.
characters.name: Elaine

characters.description: A local faction leader who took power after the king was injured. The leader can replicate items and retains police support by providing ammunition.
characters.name: Local coup leader

characters.description: The revealed identity of the shapeshifting enemy that imitates Lyra and confronts Randidly during his battle with the Frostbite judgment.
characters.name: Tribulation of Many Faces

characters.description: The local powerholder who oversees the Sanctuary's protected, hierarchical community. The leader defends the deposed king's silencing and acknowledges holding the former police chief against his will.
characters.name: Sanctuary leader

characters.description: The pre-System film sound grip known to Lyra is revealed to be Donnyton's smith Sam. He rescued Lyra and several others when the System arrived and later joined Randidly's early survivor group.
characters.name: Sam Hoss

characters.description: The outwardly friendly leader of a ragged survivor group encountered by the Frankfurd convoy. He asks for help reaching supposedly wounded companions, and Randidly accompanies him because the group's story and interest in the convoy's equipment seem suspicious.
characters.name: Desmond

characters.description: An unnamed Donnyton fighter who removes a defeated raid boss's soul and turns its beetle body into a controlled, undead-like asset.
characters.name: Donnyton ghost wielder

characters.description: A self-styled hero known only through Desmond's unverified account. Desmond claims Roy is north of the Badlands with Ace and Randidly's other missing friend, but nothing at this point independently confirms Roy's identity, conduct, or companions.
characters.name: Roy

characters.description: A strategist and representative of Donnyton's non-classed residents. After a costly assault, she questions whether classless leaders can responsibly continue without classes.
characters.name: Mrs. Hamilton

characters.description: An unnamed classless combatant who fights with an iron ball. During Donnyton's raid-force trial, he completes a path and acquires stamina-related willpower abilities.
characters.name: Iron-ball fighter

characters.description: A rabbit-like representative from another world who reaches Donnyton seeking its leaders and a goodwill exchange.
characters.name: Rabbit-like envoy

characters.description: A hunted royal cub that Dozer encounters during a prescribed pet mission. Rather than follow the expected route, he abandons the mission's instructions to rescue it, opening the possibility that the survivor may become his companion.
characters.name: Riverdrop Puma

characters.description: A Frankfurd agent sent to assess Donnyton's strength. His danger-sensing ability helps him survive the journey, but he mistakes the settlement's burning-leaf tree for a threat.
characters.name: Frankfurd investigator

characters.description: A turtle elder who shares authority in the sister village and helps decide whether its leaders will cooperate with Donnyton.
characters.name: Turtle elder

characters.description: A Sanctuary elder who regards the Tribulation's clone as her son and uses a barrier skill to protect it when Randidly confronts them.
characters.name: Sanctuary elder

characters.description: A local boat owner who explains Katal's tassel ranks and dangers, then evaluates Randidly's spear ability.
characters.name: Local boat owner

characters.description: A struggling Katal armor merchant who accepts Randidly's engraved bracers and becomes his business partner.
characters.name: Local armor merchant

characters.description: A Thundercrash disciple who wields two spears and controls a sparsely occupied qualifying platform. Randidly challenges him for a place in the Northern Regional Tournament.
characters.name: Dual-spear fighter

characters.description: A red-haired spear fighter who enters Randidly's qualifying platform and prevents surrounding contestants from interfering with his duel. She presents herself as another contender who intends to face the winner rather than accept an easy place.
characters.name: Red-haired spear fighter

characters.description: A boastful Heavy Iron disciple who enters Randidly's qualifying stage without grasping the strength of its three leading contestants. Their pressure and Randidly's pollen incapacitate him before he can establish himself in the main contest.
characters.name: Heavy Iron disciple

characters.description: The name Randidly gives his female spear rival for himself after all three surviving contestants on their platform qualify for the Northern Regional Tournament. He has also used it to identify the engraved goods sold through his local merchant.
characters.name: Ghosthound

characters.description: An experienced spear fighter who joins Shal in training Randidly. His practical instruction emphasizes balance, repetition, and muscle-memory exercises alongside the Spear Phantom style's inherited images.
characters.name: Veteran instructor

characters.description: A capable Badlands fighter who offers service as Randidly's spear attendant in exchange for a place in the prison breakout. He joins the group as it moves toward the guarded portal under the protection of shadow engravings.
characters.name: Blindfolded spear attendant

characters.description: Tomcat's leading spear fighter, who confronts the escaping prisoners. He accepts Shal's promise to return in six months and answer a life-debt for the guards killed.
characters.name: Tomcat first spear

characters.description: A Crashing Wave style artisan and business contact of the local engraver. The artisan receives Randidly's engraved armor and proposes telling him about the origins of Shal's style.
characters.name: Crashing Wave artisan

characters.description: A determined Central Region spear user and father of two sons, including Shal. After the mother of his children is murdered, he flees north with both boys.
characters.name: Spear Phantom founder

characters.description: The name of Randidly's male spear attendant, revealed when the boat owner addresses him during training. Formerly a Heavy Iron disciple, he continues challenging stronger companions while developing the defensive image behind Bulwark of Iron.
characters.name: Tommy

characters.description: A Thunderhammer style artisan retained by the Merchant Association. He leads seven spear users in boarding the boat to retaliate for a displaced merchant's lost goods and faces Randidly while Shal is unavailable.
characters.name: Thunderhammer artisan

characters.description: A Donnyton girl held in the Sanctuary with the missing rabbit ambassador. Her disappearance leads Alana to trace both captives toward the rival settlement.
characters.name: Kidnapped Donnyton girl

characters.description: The red-haired qualifier who asks to serve Randidly for a year after earning a place in the Northern Regional Tournament. Randidly accepts her offer alongside that of another former contestant.
characters.name: Woman spear attendant

characters.description: The former Heavy Iron disciple who asks to serve Randidly for a year after the qualifier. Randidly accepts him as an attendant alongside the red-haired contestant.
characters.name: Male spear attendant

characters.description: The veteran spear fighter who joins the outside team traveling to Tomcat to help free Shal and Randidly. He believes he can delay the settlement's defenders while the prisoners escape.
characters.name: Old master
